name: Apartment application fee
summary: A non-refundable fee charged by landlords or leasing agents to process a rental application, typically $25-$100 per applicant, covering credit/background-check cost plus margin.

evidence.claim: White House January 2023 fact sheet targeting rental housing junk fees including application fees, with CFPB and FTC coordinated enforcement actions.
CFPB 2024 report on rental-application-fee pricing: surveyed fees 2019-2023 rose from median $35 to median $55; typical underlying cost (credit + background check) is $15-$25.

State law variance: Washington state caps application fees at actual cost of screening + $75 max (RCW 59.18.257); California requires refund of unused portion (Civil Code §1950.6); many states have no cap.

body.description: A non-refundable fee assessed at the start of the rental application process. Justified as covering credit-report and background-check costs (~$20 combined), fees frequently run $50-$100 with the remainder being landlord margin. Some properties charge per-applicant fees even for co-applicants who share one household credit report.

identificationSignals.description: Application form charging separate fee per co-applicant rather than household-level screening.

disclosureLegality.requirement: FTC Rule-making in process on rental fee disclosure; White House 2023 fact sheet commits agencies to coordinated enforcement (E1).
RCW 59.18.257 caps application fees at screening cost + $75 (E3).
Civ. Code §1950.6 caps application screening fee at actual cost + adjusted $30 (2024 figure), and requires itemized refund of unused portion.

negotiability.waivableOnRequest: sometimes (on slow markets or end-of-month deadline pressure)
negotiability.script: Ask the leasing office: 'Can you waive the application fee since I have a credit report I can share directly?' This works most often in slow rental markets (winter months) and with smaller landlords. Corporate-owned large complexes rarely waive. If the market allows, request a portable tenant-screening report (available from TransUnion SmartMove, $40 one-time) that multiple landlords accept instead of paying per-landlord.
